meaning: The ultimate Polish swear word. Originally meaning 'whore', it functions as 'fuck', 'shit', 'bitch', and as a universal punctuation mark.
cultural_context: Used for literally any emotion: anger, joy, pain, surprise, or just to pause and think mid-sentence. Often softened to 'kurczę', 'kurde', or 'kurna' in polite company.
example_usage: Kurwa, znowu pada! (Fuck, it's raining again!)

english_equivalent: fuck off / get the fuck out
meaning: A harsh command telling someone to leave immediately or to run away very fast.
cultural_context: Derived from 'pierdolić'. Used aggressively towards someone annoying or threatening.
example_usage: Spierdalaj stąd! (Get the fuck out of here!)

meaning: To make someone extremely angry or annoyed.
cultural_context: From the root 'kurwa'. Very common way to complain about an annoying person or situation.
example_usage: Nie wkurwiaj mnie. (Don't piss me off.)

meaning: A strong, direct personal insult directed at a male. Combining 'z' (from), 'kurwa' (whore), and 'syn' (son).
cultural_context: Can start a fight if directed at a stranger. Between close male friends, it might be used admiringly ('You brilliant son of a bitch').
example_usage: Ty jebany skurwysynu! (You fucking son of a bitch!)

meaning: To have sex, to talk absolute nonsense, or to ignore/disregard something entirely.
cultural_context: One of the most versatile roots. 'Pierdolę to' means 'I'm abandoning this / Fuck this'.
example_usage: Co ty pierdolisz? (What the fuck are you talking about?)

meaning: To copulate, to beat someone up, or to hold something in contempt.
cultural_context: Often abbreviated to 'JP' (Jebać Policję - Fuck the Police) in graffiti and rap culture. 'Jebać to' means 'Fuck it / Forget about it'.
example_usage: Jebać to wszystko. (Fuck it all.)

meaning: Vulgar term for penis. Used to call someone a prick, to express hopelessness, or as an intensifier.
cultural_context: Can mean 'nothing' (Gówno i chuj - shit and nothing). 'W chuj' means 'a fucking lot'. Often misspelled as 'huj' by internet users but properly spelled with 'ch'.
example_usage: Taki z niego chuj. (He's such a dick.)

meaning: Describes something that is incredibly good, cool, or awesome.
cultural_context: From the root 'jebać'. While technically a swear word, it's used so often to mean 'awesome' that youth consider it almost standard slang. Still unacceptable in essays or at work.
example_usage: Ale zajebiście! (That's fucking awesome!)

english_equivalent: to act crazy / to act the fool
meaning: To behave erratically, strangely, or to do something completely crazy and unexpected.
cultural_context: Commonly asked when someone is doing something weird: 'Co ty odpierdalasz?' (What the fuck are you doing?)
example_usage: Odpierdala mu. (He's losing his fucking mind / acting crazy.)

english_equivalent: to work one's ass off / to run fast
meaning: To work incredibly hard, to walk/run very fast, or to steal something.
cultural_context: A blue-collar staple. Describes grueling physical labor or someone zooming by in a speeding car.
example_usage: Zapierdalam w robocie jak wół. (I'm working my fucking ass off like an ox at work.)

meaning: An extremely aggressive command to leave.
cultural_context: Similar to 'spierdalać' but often used when throwing someone physically out of a building or a group.
example_usage: Wypierdalaj z mojego domu! (Get the fuck out of my house!)

meaning: Describes an object that is broken, a situation that is ruined, or a person who is mentally deficient.
cultural_context: Used constantly for malfunctioning technology or bad weather. Derived from 'jebać'.
example_usage: Znowu ten zjebany telefon nie działa. (This fucked up phone isn't working again.)

meaning: Vulgar word for female genitalia. Used to describe a coward, a weak man, or a miserable situation.
cultural_context: Strongly offensive. When referring to weather ('pizga' - root variant), it means 'it's fucking freezing/windy'. Describing a man as 'pizda' attacks his masculinity heavily.
example_usage: Ale z ciebie pizda. (You're such a fucking pussy.)

meaning: Describes something going badly, feeling unwell, or looking terrible.
cultural_context: The direct opposite of 'zajebiście' (awesome). 'Czuję się chujowo' means 'I feel like shit.'
example_usage: Wyszło trochę chujowo. (It turned out kinda shitty.)

english_equivalent: to steal / to kill / to hit
meaning: Depends on context: 1) to steal something, 2) to murder someone, 3) to punch someone hard.
cultural_context: A very heavy verb used in a street context to describe crimes or severe physical violence.
example_usage: Ktoś mi zajebał rower. (Somebody fucking stole my bike.)

meaning: To get highly intoxicated on alcohol (or drugs).
cultural_context: Vodka culture staple word. 'Najebany' means 'wasted'. A weekend badge of honor for youths.
example_usage: Wczoraj się strasznie najebałem. (I got so fucked up yesterday.)

meaning: Literally feces. Used to mean something is worthless, or as an expression of disagreement ('bullshit').
cultural_context: Relatively mild compared to the big five. Sometimes used by children, though still scolded. 'Gówno prawda' means 'bullshit/not true at all'.
example_usage: Gówno wiesz. (You know shit / nothing.)

meaning: The buttocks. Used to describe an attractive woman, a failure, or the middle of nowhere.
cultural_context: Very mild. 'Ciemna dupa' (dark ass) means the middle of out-of-nowhere. 'Dupa wołowa' (beef ass) means a clumsy/useless person.
example_usage: Wszystko poszło do dupy. (Everything went to shit/ass.)

meaning: Describes a person who is crazy, psychotic, or completely illogical.
cultural_context: Often applied to bizarre rules, insane drivers, or unhinged people. A 'pojeb' (noun) is a crazy person.
example_usage: On jest jakiś pojebany. (He is somehow fucked up/crazy.)

meaning: A floor-rag. Metaphorically used to describe a promiscuous woman or a person of extremely low moral character.
cultural_context: Highly derogatory when applied to a woman. When applied to a man, implies he is a backstabbing, spineless scumbag.
example_usage: Zwykła szmata z niej. (She's just a common slut/rag.)

meaning: Literal word for a female sex worker, heavily used as a slur.
cultural_context: Similar to 'kurwa' but primarily maintains its original literal/insult meaning rather than acting as a comma.
example_usage: Ty jebana dziwko! (You fucking whore!)

meaning: A deeply derogatory prison slang term for a man who is forced to perform sexual acts.
cultural_context: EXTREMELY offensive, likely to cause immediate physical violence if directed at a man. It strips an individual of all masculine dignity.
example_usage: Zamilcz cwelu. (Silence, you prison bitch.)

meaning: Common homophobic slur for a gay man.
cultural_context: Often used in homophobic contexts, though sometimes used casually among straight male youths to mock effeminate behavior. Highly offensive.
example_usage: [Homophobic slur omitted in translation directly, context recognized]

meaning: To squeal or snitch on someone to a boss or authorities; alternatively, to steal something quietly.
cultural_context: Snitching ('podpierdalanie') is heavily frowned upon in Polish society, a lingering trait from resistance against historical occupiers and communist police.
example_usage: Ktoś mnie podpierdolił do szefa. (Somebody fucking snitched on me to the boss.)

english_equivalent: to destroy / to smash to pieces
meaning: To break or smash something totally, or to beat a person severely.
cultural_context: Used to describe cars totaled in accidents, or an emotional breakdown.
example_usage: Rozjebałem auto na drzewie. (I totally fucked/destroyed the car on a tree.)

meaning: Usually used as 'mieć wyjebane' (to have it fucked out), meaning to completely not care about something.
cultural_context: The ultimate expression of apathy or deciding not to stress over an impossible situation.
example_usage: Mam na to wyjebane. (I don't give a single fuck about it.)

meaning: To strike someone or something with great force or to accidentally bash oneself into an object.
cultural_context: Used when bumping a toe into a table, or punching someone in a bar.
example_usage: Przypierdoliłem głową w szafkę. (I bashed my fucking head into the cabinet.)

english_equivalent: to down (food) / to blow / to scam
meaning: Highly context-dependent: 1) to eat something very fast (ojebać kebsa), 2) to scam someone out of money, 3) to give a blowjob (ojebać gałę).
cultural_context: Demonstrates the insane versatility of Polish prefixes. The same verb means eating kebab and performing oral sex.
example_usage: Ojebałem całego kebsa. (I scarfed down the whole fucking kebab.)

meaning: To deliver a brutal verbal comeback (roast), to add an excessive amount of something, or to assign someone a terrible task.
cultural_context: 'Dojebałeś do pieca' (You added to the furnace) means you said an absolute banger of a joke or roast.
example_usage: Ale mu dojebałeś! (Man, you totally roasted him!)

meaning: Used to describe something annoying, difficult, or sometimes impressive (like 'fucking genius').
cultural_context: Commonly precedes insults (jebany skurwysyn). If someone does a magic trick well: 'Ale jebany!' (He's fucking unbelievable!).
example_usage: Ten jebany telewizor nie działa. (This fucking TV doesn't work.)

english_equivalent: to sell out / to compromise one's morals
meaning: To abandon one's principles for money or status.
cultural_context: Used to describe politicians who switch parties for cash, or indie bands that go mainstream purely for money.
example_usage: Skurwił się dla kasy. (He sold himself out for the money.)

meaning: A noun deriving from 'zjebać'. Describes a hopelessly incompetent, stupid, or annoying person.
cultural_context: Common insult for people making stupid mistakes repeatedly or having terrible opinions online.
example_usage: Co za zjeb. (What a fucking retard/moron.)

meaning: A noun defining a situation of hopelessness, poverty, bad quality, or a boring event.
cultural_context: A classic description of post-communist gloom or just a really boring party.
example_usage: Totalna chujnia z grzybnią. (A total shitty mess with mushroom-mold.)

english_equivalent: to beat the shit out of / to devour
meaning: To beat someone violently and win a fight easily, OR to eat a meal ravenously.
cultural_context: A hooligan term for beating someone up ('dostać wpierdol' = to get an ass/shit-beating).
example_usage: Zaraz ci wpierdolę. (I'm gonna beat the shit out of you right now.)

english_equivalent: shock the shit out of / to be tortured
meaning: Often used in the phrase 'takich to tylko jebać prądem' (those kinds of people should only be shocked with electricity).
cultural_context: A darkly humorous condemnation of people who are inexplicably stupid or annoying.
example_usage: Za takie zachowanie to jebać prądem. (For behavior like that, they should be electro-fucked.)

meaning: Being in a state of high anger or irritation.
cultural_context: The standard vulgar way to express that you are having a terrible, rage-inducing day.
example_usage: Jestem tak wkurwiony, że zaraz wybuchnę. (I am so pissed off I'm gonna explode.)

english_equivalent: to steal / to steal an idea / to snitch
meaning: A direct synonym to 'podpierdolić' - to sneakily steal an object, or to report someone to an authority.
cultural_context: Used for petty theft like a stolen pen at work, or taking someone's joke.
example_usage: Ktoś mi podjebał zapalniczkę. (Someone snatched my fucking lighter.)

english_equivalent: to mix up / to mess up
meaning: To confuse things, to make a total mess in someone's head, or to go crazy ('popierdoliło cię?' = have you lost your mind?).
cultural_context: A question to someone proposing a terrible or dangerous idea.
example_usage: Ciębie już do reszty popierdoliło? (Have you completely lost your fucking mind?)

meaning: A situation presenting high risk of getting caught by police/parents, or simply an embarrassing situation.
cultural_context: Very common youth slang. Not technically a swear word, but frequently paired with them.
example_usage: Ale przypał. (What a bust / How embarrassing.)

meaning: A person of no moral value. A scumbag.
cultural_context: Calling a human 'trash' is a very severe character insult in Poland, often combined with 'jebany' (fucking trash).
example_usage: Jebany śmieć. (Fucking piece of trash.)

meaning: Refers to menstruation, a gay man, or a weak, effeminate man.
cultural_context: Used to attack a man's courage or strength. 'Nie bądź ciotą' = Don't be a pussy.
example_usage: Zachowujesz się jak ciota. (You're acting like a pussy/faggot.)

meaning: Describes a person who is extremely drunk or high.
cultural_context: The most common vulgar way to describe being highly intoxicated on Friday nights.
example_usage: Jestem tak najebany, że nic nie widzę. (I'm so wasted I can't see anything.)

meaning: Synonym for drunk, slightly less vulgar than 'najebany'.
cultural_context: Used casually to recount party stories among friends.
example_usage: Przyszedł kompletnie napruty. (He arrived completely wasted.)

english_equivalent: to puke / to be sick of
meaning: Literal vomiting or expressing extreme disgust/boredom with a topic.
cultural_context: Mild vulgarity, often used metaphorically. 'Rzygam tym' means 'I am so sick of this'.
example_usage: Rzygać mi się chce jak na niego patrzę. (It makes me want to puke when I look at him.)

meaning: Diminutive of 'dupa' (ass). A selfish, mean, or arrogant man.
cultural_context: Fairly mild. Common in dubbed American movies to translate 'asshole' or 'jerk' because it avoids the harsher R-rated curses.
example_usage: Ale z niego dupek. (He's such an asshole/jerk.)

meaning: A person with an empty head; totally stupid or ignorant.
cultural_context: Won Polish 'Youth Word of the Year'. A very popular, relatively clean slang word for an idiot.
example_usage: Zamilcz dzbanie. (Be quiet, you jug/idiot.)

meaning: Used to denote 'extremely' or 'intensely'.
cultural_context: Amplifies an adjective greatly. E.g. 'kurewsko zimno' means 'fucking freezing'.
example_usage: Jest tu kurewsko zimno. (It's fucking freezing in here.)

meaning: To make a terrible mistake, completely ruin a project, or scold someone severely.
cultural_context: Universally used in casual Polish to admit a mistake ('zjebałem' - I fucked up).
example_usage: Totalnie to zjebałem. (I completely fucked this up.)

english_equivalent: to fail (an exam) / to chop off / to stain
meaning: To fail a student at an exam, to cut a limb off, or to stain your shirt with food.
cultural_context: Student slang for failing ('profesor mnie upierdolił' - the professor failed/fucked me). Also used when a saw cuts off a finger.
example_usage: Upierdoliłem stół sosem. (I stained the fucking table with sauce.)

meaning: A physical beating. Getting pummeled.
cultural_context: Associated with soccer hooligans (kibole) or bar fights. Giving someone 'wpierdol' means beating them badly.
example_usage: Chcesz dostać wpierdol? (Do you want to get your ass kicked?)

english_equivalent: to dress to impress / to execute / to do something crazy
meaning: To dress extremely elegantly ('odjebany jak stróż w Boże Ciało'), to kill someone, or to do something bizarre.
cultural_context: Highly context dependent depending on the preposition used.
example_usage: Ale się odjebałeś! (You're dressed fucking great/dressed to the nines!)

meaning: A grotesque, usually aging or very cheap prostitute/promiscuous woman.
cultural_context: A creative variation of 'kurwa' adding a judgmental and somewhat comical/disgusting tone.
example_usage: Stary kurwiszon. (Old worn-out slut.)

meaning: Describes a man who is cowardly, weak, and pathetic.
cultural_context: A direct attack on confidence and masculinity.
example_usage: Jakiś taki pizdowaty jest. (He's just sort of wimpy and pathetic.)

meaning: Direct translation of son of a bitch (suka = bitch, in = 's, syn = son).
cultural_context: Used in TV and literature to replace the harsher 'skurwysyn'. Considered slightly archaic or imported from English movie translations.
example_usage: Złapcie tego sukinsyna. (Catch that son of a bitch.)

meaning: Describes something of very poor quality, or terrible weather.
cultural_context: A fundamental descriptor in Poland. Can describe cars, movies, food, government, or life.
example_usage: Ten film był chujowy. (That movie was totally shitty.)

meaning: Variant of skurwysyn. Represents a ruthless, cunning, or bad guy.
cultural_context: Sometimes used with grudging respect for a tough villain or a very difficult task/exam.
example_usage: Twardy z niego skurwiel. (He's a tough motherfucker.)

meaning: Used to describe a very short person or an annoying, insignificant little guy.
cultural_context: Derogatory regarding height and status. From the root to fart (pierdzieć).
example_usage: Mały wypierdek. (Little runt/fart.)

meaning: A softened, family-friendly version of 'zapierdalać' (to work hard, to run fast).
cultural_context: Uses 'pieprz' (pepper) instead of 'pierdol'. Safe to say around parents and children.
example_usage: Muszę zapieprzać do pracy. (I gotta hustle/speed to work.)

meaning: A family friendly version of 'wkurwiony'. Means 'angry'.
cultural_context: Very common, essentially just standard language for being very angry, avoiding the 'kurwa' root by replacing it with 'kurz' (dust).
example_usage: Jestem mega wkurzony. (I'm super pissed/angry.)

meaning: Vulgar term for diarrhea, often used to describe rushing or panicking uselessly.
cultural_context: When a company panics to meet a deadline, employees call it 'sraczka' (panic diarrhea).
example_usage: Mają sraczkę z tym projektem. (They are shitting themselves rushing over this project.)

meaning: A sycophant. Someone who flatters bosses to get ahead.
cultural_context: Highly looked down upon in the workplace. Implies someone is literally crawling into the boss's ass.
example_usage: Straszliwy dupowlaz. (A terrible brown-noser.)

english_equivalent: to fail spectacularly / to throw a fit
meaning: To completely fail a task, or when an engine breaks down ('auto się zesrało'). Also means throwing an internet tantrum.
cultural_context: Common internet slang ('O Boże o kurwa zesrał się' meme). Means losing composure completely.
example_usage: Pewnie się zesra na widok cen. (He'll probably shit himself looking at the prices.)

meaning: Extreme malice, injustice, or widespread corruption. Sometimes refers to prostitution.
cultural_context: Used to describe an unfair system, steep taxes, or someone's malicious betrayal.
example_usage: Zwykłe kurewstwo i złodziejstwo. (Pure fucking injustice and thievery.)

meaning: Expression of abandoning a task or dismissing a problem.
cultural_context: The working man's philosophy for letting go of unfixable problems.
example_usage: Nie naprawię tego, chuj w to. (I can't fix this, fuck it.)

meaning: A person who snitches or steals small items.
cultural_context: The absolute worst kind of person in school or a factory.
example_usage: Nie ufaj mu, to podpierdalacz. (Don't trust him, he's a snitch.)

meaning: Used when something heavily breaks, collapses, or hits the ground.
cultural_context: Famous meme phrase: 'jebło to jebło, na chuj drążyć temat' (it broke, it broke, why the fuck dwell on it).
example_usage: Jebło o ziemię. (It smashed to the fucking ground.)

meaning: A person whose actions defy logic and are severely crazy.
cultural_context: Used for road-ragers, internet stalkers, or eccentric madmen.
example_usage: Zostaw go, to niezły pojeb. (Leave him be, he's quite the psycho.)

meaning: A malicious, cunning, or bad-tempered woman. Also slang for a police van.
cultural_context: In Polish rap and street slang, police vans are called 'suki' (bitches) patrolling the streets. Highly offensive towards women.
example_usage: Zabrali go do suki. (They took him to the police van-bitch.) / Wredna suka. (Evil bitch.)

meaning: A comical insult for someone who is very small, very loud, and thinks they are important.
cultural_context: Often aimed at children, young siblings, or short aggressive men.
example_usage: Cicho bądź, marny wypierdku mamuta. (Be quiet, you pitiful little mammoth fart.)

meaning: To smell incredibly bad.
cultural_context: Mild slang for expressing disgust at an odor.
example_usage: Ale tu zaśmierdziało. (God, it stinks fucking bad in here.)

meaning: A disrespectful term for a child, teenager, or immature young adult.
cultural_context: Used heavily by older generations to demean youths who mock authority.
example_usage: A co ty wiesz, gówniarzu? (What do you know, you little shit?)

meaning: A vulgar term for someone's face or mouth.
cultural_context: Telling someone 'zamknij ryj' (shut your snout) is highly aggressive and disrespectful.
example_usage: Zamknij ten głupi ryj. (Shut your stupid snout.)
